sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The white cross is gone. The purple square moved from (58-60, 3-5) to (52-54, 9-11). The path extended with new gray dots at (55,8) and (57,6).
Now I need to understand the mechanic better. The purple square moved along the diagonal path. The path direction is going up-right (decreasing row, increasing col). The next step would be to click on the next position along the path.
Looking at the path dots:
- Current purple square at (52-54, 9-11), center ~ (53, 10)
- Next dot on path going up-right would be at... let me trace the pattern
- Dots: (51,12), (49,14), (47,16), (45,18), ... going up-right
So the next position after the purple square would be (51, 12) which is the next gray dot. But wait, the purple square is now at (52-54, 9-11), and the gray dot at (51,12) is right next to it.
I think the mechanic is: click on the gray dot next to the purple square to move it along the path. The goal is to move the purple square all the way to the blue circle.
Let me try clicking on the next gray dot at (51, 12).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The purple square moved to (52-54, 9-11). The path goes up-right.
# Next gray dot on the path is at (51, 12). Let me click there to move the purple square further.
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 51, 'col': 12}])
print(f"Result: {result}")
print(f"Board changed: {result.get('board_changed')}")
print(f"Gameplay changed: {result.get('gameplay_changed')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
